name: Las Matas, Cerceda, Colmenar Viejo – circular route
name_original: Las Matas, Cerceda, Colmenar Viejo – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Las Rozas de Madrid offer varied terrain, from urban and countryside sections to continuous climbs. The region is situated within a rich natural setting, bordering the Regional Park of the Middle Course of the Guadarrama River and the Biosphere Reserve of the Upper Basins of the Manzanares, Lozoya, and Guadarrama Rivers. Cyclists can expect diverse ecosystems, including riparian forests, oak groves, and dehesas, with proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama providing access to challenging mountain passes. Best road cycling routes around Las Rozas de Madrid The most popular road cycling route is Las Matas, Cerceda, Colmenar Viejo – circular route, a 41.6 miles (67.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns, offering a mix of landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Manzanares el Real Castle – Santillana Reservoir loop from Pinar de Las Rozas, a difficult 59.5 miles (95.8 km) path. This route features scenic views of a historic castle and a reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Manzanares el Real Castle loop from Pinar de Las Rozas, a 65.8 miles (105.9 km) trail leading through elevated terrain and past a historic castle, often completed in about 4 hours 52 minutes. Road cycling around Las Rozas de Madrid is defined by its varied terrain, including riverside forests along the Guadarrama River, oak groves, and challenging ascents towards the Sierra de Guadarrama. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult mountain passes. The routes in Las Rozas de Madrid are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 3,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Las Rozas de Madrid's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Las Rozas de Madrid?
A: There are over 55 road cycling routes accessible from Las Rozas de Madrid, catering to various skill levels. These include 4 easy routes, 33 moderate routes, and 18 difficult routes, offering a wide range of options for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Las Rozas de Madrid?
A: Road cycling routes in Las Rozas de Madrid offer varied terrain, from urban and countryside sections to continuous climbs. You'll find diverse ecosystems, including riparian forests along the Guadarrama River, oak groves, and dehesas. The proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama also provides access to challenging mountain passes for more experienced riders.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural features along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints and natural features. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. During the rainy season, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is particularly impressive. Routes also pass by the unfinished 18th-century Presa El Gasco and along the Guadarrama River with its beautiful riverside forests.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while road cycling?
A: Many routes feature interesting landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24941325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle – Santillana Reservoir loop from Pinar de Las Rozas&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of a historic castle and a reservoir. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Manzanares el Real Castle loop from Pinar de Las Rozas&lt;/a&gt;, also leads past this historic castle. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; on other routes.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Las Rozas de Madrid?
A: The area sees significant cycling activity, especially in September and October, suggesting these are excellent months. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter, though winter routes are also possible.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes offer varied terrain, some are more suitable for families. The region has 4 easy routes, which are generally flatter and shorter, making them more accessible for family outings. Look for routes that avoid significant elevation gains and are away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977459870/las-matas-cerceda-colmenar-viejo-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Matas, Cerceda, Colmenar Viejo – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 41.6-mile (67.0 km) trail connecting several towns. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Bear Statue – Villanueva de la Cañada loop from Las Rozas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Las Rozas de Madrid?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes everything from riverside forests to challenging ascents, and the diverse natural settings like oak groves and dehesas. The extensive network of routes catering to different ability levels is also a highlight.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes using public transport or find parking easily?
A: Las Rozas de Madrid is well-connected to broader cycling networks, including the CiclaMadrid project, which can be accessed by train from Madrid. For those driving, many routes start from areas like Pinar de Las Rozas, where parking is generally available, though it's always advisable to check specific starting points for parking facilities.

Q: Are there any routes that connect to the Anillo Verde Ciclista (Green Cycling Ring) around Madrid?
A: Yes, the Anillo Verde Ciclista (Green Cycling Ring) around Madrid is accessible from Las Rozas, offering a long-distance cycling option. The southwestern side of the Anillo Verde Ciclista is particularly recommended and easily reachable from the area.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park means access to challenging mountain passes for more experienced road cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24941325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle – Santillana Reservoir loop from Pinar de Las Rozas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Manzanares el Real Castle loop from Pinar de Las Rozas&lt;/a&gt; are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of urban and countryside sections?
A: Yes, many routes around Las Rozas de Madrid are designed to offer a blend of urban and countryside environments. This allows cyclists to experience both the local towns and the natural beauty of the surrounding areas, including riparian forests and dehesas, often transitioning smoothly between the two.
tips: Probably its star monument due to its strange and unusual existence is the Entreterminos dolmen, an authentic megalith dating from around the 4th millennium BC. c.
It maintains a careful historic center. The most notable monuments are the church of San Miguel Arcángel, the Plaza de la Constitución, the town hall and the modern hermitage of Santa Cruz.

Colmenar Viejo is one of the most important towns in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. It is surrounded by large cattle farms and meadows. In addition, it is a crossroads of cycling routes in all its modalities.
Factory bridge over the Manzanares on the M-618 between Colmenar Viejo and Hoyo del Manzanares. Wonderful views

The town is very charming with its stone houses and is full on weekends with people hiking, climbing and biking. In the square there is an Information Office where you can get a wide variety of information.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Colmenar Viejo
